name: GoA Design System Principles
summary: Core design and development principles for teams building with the GoA Design System

corePrinciples.description: All design decisions must prioritize citizen and worker needs over aesthetic preferences
corePrinciples.application: Choose components and patterns based on user research and government service requirements

corePrinciples.description: WCAG 2.2 AA compliance is non-negotiable, not an add-on
corePrinciples.application: Use GoA components which provide built-in accessibility features

corePrinciples.description: Consistent user experience across government services trumps unique design
corePrinciples.application: Use established GoA patterns rather than inventing new ones

corePrinciples.description: Exhaust GoA component library options before building custom components
corePrinciples.application: Search the component library, check examples, combine existing components creatively

componentFirst.rule: Always start with GoA components until those options are fully exhausted before doing any custom components
process: Search for GoA components that solve your need
If no component found: Ask the team before building custom

spacingStrategy.rule: Use component margin props (mb, mr) as the primary spacing approach
options: Component margin props (mb, mr, mt, ml) - preferred

validationChecklist: Verify component properties against documented valid values
Check design specifications for exact sizing
Ensure no custom styling - only use component props
Validate spacing tokens match design system

userTypeGuidance.reference: See design/user-types.json for detailed citizen vs worker patterns
userTypeGuidance.keyInsight: Usage frequency matters more than role - a contractor filing daily permits needs efficiency patterns, not guided flows
